title: mdtoc slugger candidate A: regex-based slugify
description: Owns projects/mdtoc/candidates/slugger_a.py. Implement slugify(text, seen) using a REGEX approach (re module). Conform to the vectors.py interface. Self-check with vectors.run_against and record passed/total in the task note. Stdlib only.
acceptance_criteria: Owns EXACTLY: projects/mdtoc/candidates/slugger_a.py. Touch no other file. Depends on T-023 (vectors.py) being done.
Implements slugify(text: str, seen: dict) -> str conforming to the interface documented in projects/mdtoc/candidates/vectors.py.

APPROACH = REGEX-BASED: use the re module (compiled patterns) to lowercase, strip punctuation / keep unicode \w chars + hyphen, collapse spaces to hyphens, and dedupe via the seen dict (-1/-2 suffixes).
Self-check: import vectors and run vectors.run_against(slugify); record the exact passed/total (and any failures) in the task handoff note.
Method must be genuinely different from candidates B (unicodedata category) and C (github-slugger port) - this is a diversity-of-method tournament, not redundancy.
stdlib only; no import of the mdtoc package or the other candidates.

SHARED CONVENTIONS (all mdtoc tasks): Python 3.9+ stdlib ONLY, no pip deps. Package dir projects/mdtoc/mdtoc/ (namespace package; __init__.py is created only by T-028, so test files must add projects/mdtoc/ to sys.path and import as `from mdtoc.parser import ...`). Tests use unittest and must pass via `python3 -m unittest discover -s projects/mdtoc/tests -t projects/mdtoc`. No file listed here may be created/edited by any other task (disjoint ownership).

notes.note: plan: implement projects/mdtoc/candidates/slugger_a.py using stdlib re module (downcase -> strip non-[\w space hyphen] via compiled regex -> replace spaces with hyphens -> naive seen-dict base/count dedup per vectors.py contract); self-check via vectors.run_against, target 24/24.

EXPECTED: regex-based slugify (lower -> re.sub strip non-[\w space hyphen] -> space->hyphen -> naive seen-dict base/count dedup) scores 24/24 on vectors.run_against. ACTUAL: ran `python3 .harness/bin/goal_mode.py run --task T-024 --cmd 'cd projects/mdtoc/candidates && python3 -c "import vectors, slugger_a, sys; p,t,f=vectors.run_against(slugger_a.slugify); print(f0)"'` -> GOAL REACHED iteration 1/3; raw self-check `cd projects/mdtoc/candidates && python3 -c "import vectors, slugger_a; print(vectors.run_against(slugger_a.slugify)[:2])"` -> passed=24 total=24, zero failures. Expected matched actual exactly, no deviations. stdlib-only (re), owns exactly slugger_a.py, no import of mdtoc package or sibling candidates.

HANDOFF -> verifier: Replay: cd '/Users/mariocasanova10pa/Documents/Universal Harness/projects/mdtoc/candidates' && python3 -c "import vectors, slugger_a, sys; p,t,f=vectors.run_against(slugger_a.slugify); print(f'passed={p} total={t}'); [print('FAIL:',c,'exp=',e,'got=',g) for c,e,g in f]; sys.exit(0 if p==t else 1)" -> observed: passed=24 total=24, zero failures, exit 0. Bounded self-check also run via goal_mode.py run --task T-024 --cmd <same, cd-relative> -> GOAL REACHED on iteration 1/3. Approach: pure re module -- str.lower() then re.compile(r'[^\w \-]', re.UNICODE).sub('') to strip non-word/space/hyphen chars without collapsing whitespace (reproduces the Foo&Bar -> foo--bar double-hyphen quirk), then literal-space-to-hyphen substitution, then naive seen[base]-count dedup (base->1 first use, base-N on Nth repeat) matching vectors.py's DEDUP_SEQUENCES exactly. File owns exactly projects/mdtoc/candidates/slugger_a.py; stdlib-only (imports only re); does not import mdtoc package, vectors is only imported by the self-check script/verifier, not by slugger_a.py itself; does not import or read slugger_b.py/slugger_c.py. No deviations from the T-024 acceptance criteria.

VERIFIED (conformance): fresh python3 -c vectors.run_against(slugger_a.slugify) -> passed=24 total=24, zero failures. Interface: inspect.signature = slugify(text: str, seen: dict) -> str; mutation probe slugify('Foo',seen) x3 -> 'foo'/'foo-1'/'foo-2' with the SAME dict object bumped seen['foo']=1->2->3, exact per vectors.py docstring. Method-authenticity: module-level import is ONLY re; _STRIP_RE=re.compile(r'[^\w \-]').sub does the core filtering, no unicodedata -> genuinely regex-core. Independence: imports no sibling candidate and no mdtoc pkg; edge probe shows A strips combining marks (Python \w excludes Mn), a genuine behavioral divergence from B/C confirming a distinct implementation, not a copy.
